contradictions.description: Clause A encourages AI for research assistance including literature discovery and summarization; Clause B states that any AI use for research activities constitutes academic misconduct.
Clause A mandates formal APA citation with model name, version, access date, and prompt; Clause B says a brief acknowledgment is fully sufficient with no formal citation required.
Clause A restricts students to MIT-approved tools only, making non-approved tools a policy violation; Clause B permits any commercially available tool provided results are independently verified.
Clause A states the policy applies uniformly to ALL MIT students including graduate students; Clause B exempts graduate students doing thesis or dissertation research from all AI restrictions.
Clause A requires students to retain complete AI interaction logs for a minimum of one academic year after submission; Clause B states no record retention obligation exists after final submission.

Clause A states first violation results in a workshop and file notation with NO GPA impact or course failure; Clause B states ANY confirmed violation including first offense results in automatic failing grade.
